title: ophrys fusca subsp. iricolor
intro: Ophrys fusca subsp. iricolor, also known as the Green Helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the healing of wounds. The Green Helleborine can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
